summary: Remote code execution backdoor: eval of payload fetched from anonymous paste host on require()
details: On module load, index.js calls getPlugin(), which fetches https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QBRMI and passes the response's `model` field directly to eval(). jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste host — the maintainer can change the executed JavaScript at any time without republishing the package. Any process that require()s this package executes whatever code is hosted at that URL at the moment of the require. The package is disguised to attract installers: package.json declares the keywords ['koa','middleware','compose'], the README advertises a dependency-management utility, and index.js bundles a near-verbatim copy of koa-compose to look like a legitimate middleware library. The compose() implementation is unrelated to the trailing remote-eval logic and exists only as cover for the backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
